jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
Executive Director RCFE

Pay Range: $130,000 - $150,000

Ivy Park at Glen Cove
is a premier senior living community located in Vallejo, California
, and managed by Oakmont Management Group.

Based in Irvine, California, Oakmont Management Group is a recognized leader in senior living, operating 110+ communities under the Oakmont Senior Living and Ivy Living brands. We are the #1 operator in California and a top ten operator in the United States, serving thousands of seniors across California, Nevada, Colorado, and Hawaii. With strong momentum and continued expansion, we are actively growing into new markets.

The Executive Director
is responsible for the efficient operations and overall management and direction of the day-to-day functions of their Community in accordance with property policies and procedures, current standards, guidelines, and regulations. They will hire, evaluate, coordinate, monitor performance, schedule, and supervise staff in accordance with company policy. The Executive Director is also responsible for achieving and maintaining budgeted occupancy; achieving and maintaining budgetary compliance; professionally representing the property to the community and the Corporation, and creating a supportive and enjoyable lifestyle for the residents.

Qualifications

Completion of two (2) years of college and at least three (3) years of experience providing residential care to the elderly, or equivalent education and experience as approved by the Department of Social Services.
A valid California Residential Care Facility for the Elderly Certification or Licensed Nursing Home Executive Director
Prefer five (5) years of experience supervising and managing employees
Able to operate a personal computer and be proficient in MS Office programs (Word, Excel, etc.)
Knowledge of and/or ability to learn the theory and practice of assisted living and dementia care
Able to work with seniors and patiently interact with cognitively impaired individuals
Able to be flexible, adapt and respond to change, make decisions in stressful situations, and prioritize tasks and projects.
Able to process information and apply common sense understanding to follow and carry out written or oral instructions.
Able to analyze, solve and respond to problems or concerns.
Able to count and perform moderately complex math problems, read and understand budgets and other financial reports/statements
Able to explain ideas and communicate complex ideas to a wide audience (employees, residents, family members, governmental agencies, public, etc) both in writing and verbally
Must pass a Criminal Background check and Health Screening tests, including physical and TB Tests
For the health and safety of our team members and residents, Oakmont Management Group may require team members to vaccinate, participate in daily screening, surveillance testing, and to wear face coverings and other personal protective equipment (PPE) to prevent the spread of the COVID-19 or other communicable diseases, per regulatory guidelines.

With communities across California,
Colorado,
Hawaii, and Nevada, opportunities for career growth, relocation, and travel are significant. In addition, eligible team members may enjoy the following benefits:

Medical, Dental, and Vision benefits
Vacation, Personal Day, Sick Pay, Holidays
Complimentary Meals
Bonus Opportunities
Company Paid Life Insurance
Team Member Discount Program (LifeMart)
401(k) Savings Plan with Company Match
Recognition Programs
Student Loan Refinancing
Pet Insurance
Employee Assistance Program
Emergency Financial Assistance

Our Culture Performance defines Oakmont’s culture. We set high standards and hold ourselves accountable to them, delivering exceptional experiences for residents, families, team members, and stakeholders. We pair that commitment with a spirit of warmth, fostering joy and genuine human connection each day.

Our team is thoughtfully selected for talent, experience, and a genuine passion for serving seniors. If you take pride in your work and strive for excellence, we invite you to apply.

Oakmont Management Group is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

jobs.title: Global Security - North America Strategy, Governance and Operations, Executive Director
jobs.description:
Job Description

The North America Corporate Security team consists of a dynamic group of security professionals who support and enable the physical security of our corporate locations and critical facilities in partnership with a broader security apparatus consisting of our security operations center, guard force management, and technical security systems, as well as other key partners such as intelligence, investigations, and Retail. This group is responsible for the end-to-end security program starting with design basis threat definition, our design of layered controls, and periodic testing and assessments.

As the Head of Strategy, Governance and Operations, on the North America Corporate Security team, you will play a key role in aligning direction and facilitating coordination across our Corporate and Technical security teams. Specifically, you will be responsible for implementing and continuing to evolve the operating model in North America, ensuring that security programs are effectively calibrated to the threats confronting different types of facility and driving consistency across the region. You will also ensure effective governance and delivery of both operations and strategic programs across Corporate and Technical security, including uplifting the performance of our guarding and ensuring preparedness for a range of security incidents.

Job Responsibilities

Build relationships with business and corporate stakeholders to support daily security operations and strategic programs and projects.
Manage organizational priorities through strategic planning, budgeting, program and project delivery, process improvement, and stakeholder reporting.
Develop and refine a cross-system strategy to mature North America corporate security, optimizing threat definition, layered controls, implementation, and periodic assessment.
Drive continuous process improvement activities which include process definition and refinement, risk identification, and control development to ensure the continuance of well-defined, measurable, auditable operations.
Develop operating and quality metrics across operational teams, the guard force program, and the technology work pipeline, using digital dashboards and analytical tools.
Lead small and large group meetings and working sessions with clear, effective communication.
Manage people, processes, and controls while partnering with the business, corporate functions, technology teams, and multiple vendor partners.

Required Qualifications, Capabilities And Skills

Significant management experience in a similarly complex public or private sector organization
Knowledge of the security industry including guard force management, technical security systems, security operations and incident management.
Strong Project Management and Operations skills required
Excellent communication (both written and oral) and organizational skills
Ability to communicate technology and security concepts to a broad range of technical and non-technical staff, to include the ability to articulate the value of physical security in a corporate environment
Intelligent, articulate, and persuasive leader who can serve as a senior leader within the North America Security management team
Strong critical thinking and problem-solving skills
Ability to prioritize workloads within a busy, changing environment and meet tight deadlines

Preferred Qualifications, Capabilities And Skills

Previous experience with strategy/ management consulting, organizational strategy, or governance related work preferred

ABOUT US

JPMorganChase, one of the oldest financial institutions, offers innovative financial solutions to millions of consumers, small businesses and many of the world's most prominent corporate, institutional and government clients under the J.P. Morgan and Chase brands. Our history spans over 200 years and today we are a leader in investment banking, consumer and small business banking, commercial banking, financial transaction processing and asset management.

We offer a competitive total rewards package including base salary determined based on the role, experience, skill set and location. Those in eligible roles may receive commission-based pay and/or discretionary incentive compensation, paid in the form of cash and/or forfeitable equity, awarded in recognition of individual achievements and contributions. We also offer a range of benefits and programs to meet employee needs, based on eligibility. These benefits include comprehensive health care coverage, on-site health and wellness centers, a retirement savings plan, backup childcare, tuition reimbursement, mental health support, financial coaching and more. Additional details about total compensation and benefits will be provided during the hiring process.

We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants' and employees' religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation.

JPMorgan Chase & Co. is an Equal Opportunity Employer, including Disability/Veterans

About The Team

Our professionals in our Corporate Functions cover a diverse range of areas from finance and risk to human resources and marketing. Our corporate teams are an essential part of our company, ensuring that we're setting our businesses, clients, customers and employees up for success.

The Global Security (GS) team protects the firm's people and assets, ensuring the safety of business operations through the implementation of technology, best-in-class talent and client collaboration. Teams are responsible for developing safety policies and procedures, customer safety, pre-employment screening, fraud investigations and security operations on a global basis.

jobs.title: Microwave Program Director
jobs.description:
TTM Technologies, Inc. – Publicly Traded US Company, NASDAQ (TTMI) – Top-5 Global Printed Circuit Board Manufacturer

About TTM

TTM Technologies, Inc. is a leading global manufacturer of technology products, including mission systems, radio frequency (“RF”) components, RF microwave/microelectronic assemblies, and technologically advanced printed circuit boards (“PCB”s). TTM stands for time-to-market, representing how TTM's time-critical, one-stop design, engineering and manufacturing services enable customers to reduce the time required to develop new products and bring them to market.

Additional information can be found at www.ttm.com

Position Summary:

The RF/Microwave Program Director is responsible for the Program Management and P&L for the RF/Microwave business within the Integrated Electronics division of TTM Technologies. This position is responsible for the execution of contract requirement and performance for the RF/Microwave businesses. Responsibilities include oversight of manufacturing, engineering, quality, facilities, purchasing, customer service, and production control as it relates to program performance and execution. The role is also responsible for strategy and growth of the RF/Microwave business including Business Development and Proposal strategy. Direct reports include a team of program management professionals. The role has matrixed support from business development/sales, engineering, manufacturing, human resources, finance, and IT.

Performs a variety of tasks, leads and directs the work of others. This position is outward facing to TTM customers and internal to TTM leadership.

The position may be remote based with travel up to 50%, onsite is preferred.

Duties and Responsibilities:

Provide the overall vision, strategy, and leadership for the business group and effectively communicates to the organization, as appropriate.
Sets the goals and objectives for the group, including orders, sales, growth, operating performance, budget, and others in support of the strategy.
Develop and communicate effective business plans and ROI analysis to support strategy and growth objectives.
Lead the development of market, product, and technology strategy which are consistent with the corporate strategic plan. Work closely with the customer base, sales, marketing, and internal engineering resources, as appropriate, to insure the strategy is sound and to develop the plans to implement them. Maintain a good understanding of the customer's new product and program development needs and insure clear dissemination of that information to appropriate personnel.
Lead a team of Program Managers to Develop and implement business plans, schedules, and budgets to meet the site goals and objectives. Participate in required company planning sessions to develop the group’s short and long-term strategies, including technology and product road-maps. Monitor and provide direction and leadership for assigned staff, to insure the progress of such plans, recommend and implement corrective actions as necessary.
Define program tasking/milestones and monitor adherence to program budgets, plans, and schedules.
Plan and allocate human and equipment resources to complete assigned objectives within schedule and budget commitments. Be responsive to customer needs and insure customer satisfaction and product quality are of the highest priority.
Insure all direct reports and functions managed actively support quality and customer satisfaction. Institute an atmosphere of continuous product improvement for all company personnel.
Maintain the necessary communications and cooperation with other company functions and business groups to support overall company goals and objectives.
Comply with and actively support Company policies and procedures such as equal employment opportunity, affirmative action, safety, ISO 9000, and ethical business practices.
Other related duties as assigned by manager.

Essential Knowledge and Skills:

Excellent communication and management skills
Program execution, preferable in the defense sector, focused on program execution, EAC’s, EVMS as applicable, and the ability to create a team focused on achieving goals
Problem solving where development of new solutions, methods, systems will result in a major impact on the company’s success.
Must be a highly organized and effective leader, communicator, decision maker and able to inspire teams to meet program objectives
Experience establishing and maintaining good working relationships with all levels of the organization, including customers
Work collaboratively within a matrixed organization to ensure the program is properly resourced at all phases of the program with personnel that have the right skill sets and company values
Ability to implement own recommendations and see projects through to completion
Make recommendations and implement improvements to processes, increasing efficiency
Strong leadership characteristics, and demonstrated talent development skills
Maintains a working knowledge of the manufacturing processes & systems in order to communicate effectively with their external and internal customers
Strong working knowledge of industry standards, certifications, and requirements
Strong business acumen and comfort in enabling, participating in, and influencing business decisions

Required Education and Experience:

Education:
Bachelors degree in Engineering, Business or related field.

Experience
: 10+ years of leadership experience in an Aerospace & Defense; manufacturing experience preferred; Experience with successful implementation of organizational change. Experience within the A&D electronics industry preferred.

#ADBU

Compensation and Benefits:

TTM offers a variety of health and well-being benefit programs. Benefit options include medical, dental, vision, 401k, flexible spending and health savings accounts, accident benefits, life insurance, disability benefits, paid vacation & holidays. Benefits are available on the 1st of the month following date of hire. For some positions, only PTO and holiday benefits are offered.

Compensation ranges for roles at TTM Technologies varies depending on a wide array of factors that are considered in making compensation decisions including but not limited to skill sets; experience and training; certifications; and other business and organizational needs. The disclosed range estimate has not been adjusted for the applicable geographic differential associated with the location at which the position may be filled. At TTM Technologies, it is not typical for an individual to be hired at or near the top of the range for their role and compensation decisions are dependent on each individual set of circumstances. A reasonable estimate of the current range is:

$131,754 - $231,705

Additional compensation: An annual or quarterly company bonus may apply based on position assignment. Positions hired for alternative shift may be eligible for a shift premium based on the assigned shift and location.

Export Statement:

Must comply with TTM Export Control Policies and Procedures and all applicable laws including ITAR, EAR and OFAC including but not limited to: a) being able to identify ITAR product on the manufacturing floor and understand that access to these products and related technical data is restricted to only US Citizens and US Permanent Residents; b) recognition of Foreign Person visitors by badge differentiation; c) understand and follow authorization procedures for bringing foreign visitors into facilities (VAL); d) understand the Export and ITAR requirements for shipments leaving the US; e) manage vendor approvals for ITAR manufacturing and services.

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, citizenship, disability or protected veteran status.

jobs.title: Right Hand to the CEO
jobs.description:
Careerscape is recruiting on behalf of our client, a fast-moving New York City company, for a sharp, resourceful Right Hand to the CEO. This is a rare entry level opportunity to sit at the center of the business, working directly with executive leadership on the priorities that matter most day to day. You will get an unfiltered view of how a company is run, from strategic planning to the smallest operational details that keep everything moving.

The day to day is fast, varied, and never quite the same from one week to the next. One moment you might be pulling together a board deck, the next you are coordinating a last minute trip or tracking down an answer the CEO needs before a call. People who thrive in this role are proactive, discreet, and energized by ambiguity, comfortable juggling multiple priorities without losing sight of what matters most, and eager to learn the business from the inside out.

Responsibilities

Manage the CEOs calendar, scheduling, and daily priorities
Prepare briefing materials, presentations, and board meeting decks
Coordinate domestic and international travel arrangements
Track action items across projects and follow up to ensure deadlines are met
Act as a liaison between the CEO and internal teams, partners, and clients
Handle confidential information with sound judgment and discretion
Research topics, competitors, and market trends as requested
Draft and proofread emails, memos, and internal communications
Support special projects and initiatives as they arise
Help organize company events, offsites, and leadership meetings

Requirements

Bachelors degree or equivalent practical experience
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast paced environment
Comfort working with ambiguity and shifting priorities
Proficiency with Microsoft Office and Google Workspace
High level of discretion when handling sensitive information
Self starter attitude with strong problem solving instincts
Availability to accommodate occasional early or late calls across time zones

Benefits

Medical, dental, and vision insurance
401k plan with company match
Paid time off and paid holidays
Home office stipend for remote equipment
Professional development and learning budget
Direct mentorship and exposure to executive leadership
Parental leave benefits
Company provided laptop and technology
Employee wellness programs

jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
MCGP Executive Director

Job Summary
: The Executive Director serves as the chief executive officer of MCGP, responsible for overseeing day-to-day operations, implementing strategic initiatives, and fostering a positive, inclusive community. The Executive Director will guide self-organizing teams, support community-led projects, and cultivate a collaborative culture. Reporting directly to the Board of Directors, this role works closely with Resident and Visiting scholars, staff, volunteers, and community members to fulfill MCGP’s mission and enhance its impact.

Key Responsibilities

Leadership and Vision
Provide visionary leadership aligned with MCGP’s mission, values, and strategic direction.
Collaborate with the Board to develop strategic plans that emphasize leadership and community empowerment.
Empower and guide self-managed teams, ensuring their goals align with MCGP’s vision while fostering distributed accountability.
Develop resources and training to enhance team-led initiatives and build capacity for distributed decision-making.

Operations Management
Oversee the administration and management of MCGP’s facilities, programs, and services, ensuring effective and efficient resource use.
Implement frameworks for team collaboration and cross-functional support.

Financial Sustainability
Develop and manage the annual budget with the finance committee to ensure financial health.
Drive fundraising, grant writing, and partnerships to support MCGP’s growth and community initiatives.

Community Engagement and Empowerment
Foster a sense of unity through open communication, inclusive programming, and by supporting community-driven projects.
Engage with community leaders, government, and partner organizations to promote collaboration and MCGP’s role as a community hub.

Human Resources and Capacity Building
Recruit, train, and supervise staff and volunteers, emphasizing skills in self-management, leadership, and project ownership.
Create a positive work environment that supports professional growth and enables team members to thrive in a distributed leadership model.

Collaborative Decision-Making
Implement processes for transparent and inclusive decision-making, incorporating input from staff, volunteers, and community members.
Build feedback channels to empower teams and ensure all voices are heard in the organization’s direction.

Education, Outreach, and Public Relations
Oversee educational programs and outreach initiatives that meet community needs and foster interfaith relationships.
Serve as the spokesperson for MCGP, promoting its values and positive image within the broader community.

Qualifications
:

Bachelor’s degree in Islamic Studies, Business Administration, Nonprofit Management, or related field (Master’s degree preferred).
Demonstrated experience in decentralized or collaborative leadership models.
Proven leadership experience in nonprofit or community-based organizations.
Strong understanding of Islamic principles and values, with sensitivity to diverse community needs.
Skilled in fundraising, grant writing, and financial management.
Excellent communication, interpersonal, and organizational skills.
Commitment to building an inclusive, collaborative, and empowered community environment.

This role is ideal for a dynamic leader passionate about community-driven growth, with a vision for empowering teams and fostering an inclusive, collaborative environment in alignment with MCGP’s mission. Please submit your resume, cover letter, and references to
Admin@themuslimcenter.org
with subject line of the position you are interested in.

Multimedia and Videos of your community engagement is also welcomed in the submission.

Please note that not all positions may be filled at the same time, but may be filled on a staggered basis based on qualifications and needs.

jobs.title: Business Development Director
jobs.description:
Job Summary

The Business Development (BD) Director is responsible for driving growth across UHY’s national service lines by expanding the firm’s footprint in local markets and promoting cross-selling opportunities within key practice areas. This role leads all aspects of the sales process, including segmented prospect targeting, identifying opportunities for service line integration, supporting partners in solution development, assembling pursuit teams, and managing the process from initial contact through to closing and contract finalization. The BD Director is expected to build and maintain a strong sales pipeline, deliver strategic presentations and proposals, and collaborate across teams to convert opportunities into new business.

In addition to direct sales responsibilities, the BD Director works closely with firm leadership to co-lead growth efforts through proactive prospecting, relationship-building, and active participation in industry and professional associations. This position requires a strategic, client-focused individual who excels at developing relationships, navigating complex business environments, and managing multiple priorities simultaneously in a fast-paced setting.

Job Description

Business Development & Strategic Growth

Increase UHY’s market presence and collaborate with leadership for a cohesive go-to-market strategy
Generate and qualify new business opportunities with prospective clients
Support Partners, Principals, and Directors in cross-selling additional services to existing clients
Identify marketing and business development initiatives to drive cross-selling and key account growth
Collaborate across teams to understand firm offerings and align them with ideal client profiles
Execute the sales process, coordinating internal and external resources to best position the firm
Leverage networking contacts, professional affiliations, industry groups, and Centers of Influence
Assist in preparing sales materials, proposals, and prospect communications
Document business development activities in Salesforce and maintain a qualified pipeline
Provide ongoing market insights to National Sales Organization management

Private Equity, M&A & Advisory Network

Established relationships with Private Equity firms, Independent Sponsors, Investment Bankers, M&A Advisors, Commercial Bankers, Transaction Attorneys, and other Centers of Influence
Proven ability to originate opportunities for Transaction Advisory Services (TAS), Quality of Earnings (QoE), Financial Due Diligence, M&A Advisory, and Technology, Risk and Compliance (TRC) engagements
Experience identifying opportunities throughout the transaction lifecycle, including buy-side, sell-side, recapitalizations, and portfolio company growth initiatives
Strong network of CFOs, Controllers, Business Owners, and Private Equity-backed portfolio company executives
Demonstrated success generating revenue through relationship-based selling within the middle-market ecosystem
Active involvement in organizations such as Association for Corporate Growth (ACG), Alliance of Mergers & Acquisitions Advisors (AM&AA), and other transaction-focused professional associations

Ideal Candidate Profile

Existing network of Private Equity firms, Investment Bankers, M&A Advisors, Attorneys, Commercial Bankers, and other Centers of Influence
Demonstrated success sourcing opportunities for Transaction Advisory Services (TAS), Technology, Risk and Compliance (TRC), CFO Advisory, Consulting, and other professional services
Ability to leverage relationships to create introductions and opportunities with middle-market companies and PE-backed organizations
Experience navigating complex sales cycles involving multiple stakeholders and high-value consulting engagements

Preferred Network Strengths

Strong connections with C-Suite executives and decision-makers
Experience working with FP&A professionals and financial strategists
Active involvement in industry organizations and professional associations
Engagement with CFOs, Controllers, and Business Owners to drive business opportunities
Understanding of and experience working with Middle Market Companies

Supervisory Responsibilities

N/A

Work environment

Work is conducted in a professional office environment with minimal distractions

Physical demands

Prolonged periods of sitting at a desk and performing work in front of a computer screen for long periods of time
Must be able to lift up to 15 pounds at a time

Travel Required

Travel required (local and overnight)

Required Education And Experience

Bachelor’s degree in business administration, finance, accounting, marketing or equivalent experience
10+ years of professional services sales experience
Understands the business issues associated with accounting and/or professional services, strong ability to uncover needs and develop solutions to client issues
Ability to establish and cultivate long-term effective relationships with internal and external relationships
Must be motivated and self-disciplined; must possess strong time management skills
Demonstrate strong communication, presentation, analytical and organizational skills
Prior success meeting and/or exceeding annual sales target
Experience leveraging a CRM tool for report generation and sales tracking
Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, and SharePoint) and Salesforce software

Preferred Education And Experience

Master’s degree in business administration, finance, accounting, marketing or equivalent experience
Prior success consultative selling for a consulting or accounting firm
Experience in professional service environment, such as a CPA firm, financial consulting firm, or similar setting
Prior FP&A experience either with software or service

Other Duties

Please note this job description is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities that are required of the colleague for this job. Duties, responsibilities and activities may change at any time with or without notice.

jobs.title: Chief Program Officer, The AI Access Initiative
jobs.description:
1. About Our Client:
The AI Access Initiative (2AI) operates within the artificial intelligence sector, focusing on leveraging AI innovations to address poverty in low- and middle-income countries. The organization aims to bridge the development gap by scaling AI-enabled programs that benefit tens or hundreds of millions of people living in poverty. It collaborates with global development actors, AI labs, and researchers to create open-sourced resources and implement impactful AI solutions in agriculture and health, with outreach already impacting millions in countries such as India.

2. About the Opportunity:
The Chief Program Officer will lead and scale programmatic efforts across agriculture and health portfolios, playing a critical role in expanding AI-enabled interventions to reach tens of millions of beneficiaries. This position involves setting program strategy, building and managing teams, and establishing systems to ensure effective program delivery and learning. As a key leadership team member, the Chief Program Officer influences the organization’s direction and represents it to partners and funders.

3. Responsibilities:
• Serve on the executive leadership team, shaping strategy, culture, and priorities.
• Hire, mentor, and develop leadership and program staff.
• Establish rigorous learning architectures with clear hypotheses, measurement, iteration, and reporting.
• Guide resource allocation based on cost-effectiveness evidence.
• Develop and maintain open-sourced playbooks and insights.
• Set and execute ambitious program strategies and expansion plans.
• Collaborate closely with regional and program leaders.
• Build program teams, systems, and partnerships to support scaling.
• Engage with funders and external stakeholders as a key organizational representative.

4. Requirements:
• Experience leading large-scale program operations, preferably across multiple geographies.
• Strong ability to recruit and develop high-performing teams.
• Expertise in designing program strategy and organizational infrastructure.
• Skills in implementing learning and measurement processes.
• Ability to work collaboratively with diverse stakeholders including governments and partners.

jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer
jobs.description:
Hospitality Management Corporation (HMC) is a Dallas-based, privately held provider of hotel management and hospitality services, delivering tailored solutions designed to meet the unique needs of each client. Our mission is to provide best-in-class management expertise with a foundation of integrity, transparency, and accountability.

At HMC, we recognize that our success is driven by our people. We are committed to fostering a culture that empowers team members, encourages growth, and provides the resources and support needed to achieve exceptional results.

We are currently searching for a Chief Operating Officer to be based in our corporate office in Lewisville, TX. The right person will be an integral part of our leadership team, overseeing our portfolio of 58 full, select, and economy branded hotels and 27 Penny’s Diner free standing restaurants.

Why Work With Us?

Family first - we believe it!
We recognize efforts and reward results
Great benefits package, medical, dental, vision, and 401K

Job Responsibilities

Provide strategic leadership and operational oversight for the company’s portfolio of hotels and restaurants, ensuring alignment with company goals, brand standards, and ownership expectations.
Directly manage and mentor company team leaders, Vice Presidents of Operation, Regional Vice Presidents, General Managers, and other leaders, fostering accountability, professional development, and high performance across all levels.
Drive operational excellence by analyzing key performance metrics (Flowthrough, GOP, guest satisfaction, labor costs) and implementing initiatives to improve profitability and efficiency.
Serve as the primary liaison to hotel ownership groups, delivering clear, data-driven presentations and updates on performance, capital projects, and strategic initiatives.
Build and maintain strong relationships with ownership stakeholders, demonstrating credibility, transparency, and a solutions-oriented approach.
Oversee budgeting, forecasting, and financial planning processes in collaboration with finance teams and property leadership.
Ensure compliance with brand standards, company policies, and all regulatory requirements across the portfolio.
Lead multi-property operational initiatives, including renovations, rebranding efforts, technology implementations, and process improvements.
Effectively prioritize and manage multiple, competing demands across a diverse portfolio, demonstrating strong organizational and decision-making skills in a fast-paced environment.
Partner cross-functionally with sales, marketing, revenue management, HR, and finance teams to drive overall business performance. of company standards.

Key Metrics for Success

Financial Performance: Achieves a Green Balanced Scorecard or better across the portfolio
Market Performance: meet or exceed the budgeted RevPAR Index (RGI) and STR results for the hotel portfolio.
Guest Experience: Guest satisfaction scores at brand average or better, with strong online reputation ratings
Operational Efficiency: Effective labor management, cost control, and consistent brand compliance
Team Leadership: High employee engagement, retention, and development of above-property leaders
Owner Satisfaction: Delivers clear communication, strong relationships, and meets or exceeds ownership objectives
Execution: Successfully leads multi-property initiatives, renovations, and operational improvements

Experience Required

Demonstrated success in full and select service hotel operations, with a proven ability to drive performance across multiple properties through strong operational leadership, management training, and hands-on task force support.
Experience leading within a regional or multi-property structure, elevating operational standards and improving overall hotel performance.
Strong relationship-building skills with corporate teams, property leadership, and ownership groups, with the ability to communicate effectively and influence stakeholders at all levels.
Proven track record in roles requiring both strategic operational leadership and hands-on, tactical execution.
Extensive hotel operations experience with multi-brand exposure, including:
Operational leadership across rooms, food & beverage, and support departments
Proven track record of financial oversight, budgeting, and cost control
Ability to lead and grow a team via training, coaching, and leadership development
Experience working with multiple ownership groups
Free standing restaurant experience a plus

Education & Other

Bachelor Degree in Business or Hotel Administration highly preferred
Industry recognition via peer groups, awards, commendations, advanced training

jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer (COO) - Virginia Community & State Medicaid Health Plan - Remote
jobs.description:
At UnitedHealthcare, we're simplifying the health care experience, creating healthier communities and removing barriers to quality care. The work you do here impacts the lives of millions of people for the better. Come build the health care system of tomorrow, making it more responsive, affordable and optimized. Ready to make a difference? Join us to start Caring. Connecting. Growing together

The Chief Operating Officer (COO) serves as a key member of the executive leadership team and is responsible for the day to day operational performance of the health plan, ensuring consistent execution of the Cardinal Care Managed Care Contract with the Virginia Department of Medical Assistance Services (DMAS). This role provides enterprise wide leadership across operations, clinical delivery support, network operations, compliance execution, and operational readiness-translating strategy into disciplined, reliable operations that deliver high quality, compliant, and member centered outcomes across the Commonwealth of Virginia.

The COO partners closely with the CEO, Chief Medical Officer, Chief Financial Officer, Chief Compliance Officer, and functional leaders to ensure operational excellence, audit readiness, and solid performance against contract, quality, financial, and stakeholder expectations under Cardinal Care.

You'll enjoy the flexibility to work remotely* from anywhere within the U.S., preferably in VA, as you will be required to travel 50% within the VA Market as you take on some tough challenges.

Primary Responsibilities

Operational Leadership & Execution

Leads relationship management on all Operations related areas for the Cardinal Care program with the DMAS and all other State government agencies who have regulatory oversight of elements of the Cardinal Care/DSNP program to influence program design, develop support for program efforts, and secure approvals
Lead and oversee core health plan operations (provider & member related, network, IT, claims, etc.) ensuring effective implementation of all Cardinal Care contract requirements across covered populations, regions, and services
Translate enterprise strategy and DMAS commitments into clear operational priorities, workflows, performance metrics, and accountability structures
Ensure operational consistency and scalability across regions while supporting local engagement and region specific execution aligned with DMAS expectations

Regulatory Compliance & Audit Readiness

Maintain operational readiness for DMAS, CMS, EQRO, operational, and financial audits, ensuring timely, accurate, and coordinated responses
Partner with Compliance and Legal to embed contract compliant processes, internal controls, and monitoring into daily operations
Support ongoing review and implementation of contract amendments and policy updates in coordination with DMAS

Clinical & Care Delivery Operations (Operational Oversight)

Provide operational oversight and support for care management, care coordination, behavioral health integration, LTSS operations, and population specific programs, ensuring alignment with Cardinal Care delivery models
Collaborate with the CMO and clinical leaders to ensure operational infrastructure supports quality improvement, clinical outcomes, and member experience goals

Network, Member & Provider Operations

Oversee operational functions supporting provider network performance, member services, grievances and appeals, enrollment support, and service delivery performance
Ensure operations are structured to support provider engagement, timely issue resolution, and service accessibility across all regions of the Commonwealth

Performance Management & Continuous Improvement

Establish and monitor operational KPIs, dashboards, and management routines tied to quality, compliance, financial stewardship, and stakeholder satisfaction
Lead continuous improvement initiatives to enhance efficiency, reduce operational risk, and improve service reliability while maintaining compliance

People, Culture & Cross Functional Leadership

Lead, mentor, and develop operational leaders, fostering a culture of accountability, collaboration, and operational excellence
Promote solid cross functional integration between operations, clinical, compliance, finance, and shared services

External & Stakeholder Engagement

Serve as a senior operational liaison to DMAS and other state partners as appropriate, supporting clear communication, issue resolution, and trusted relationships in partnership with the CEO and executive team
Build, develop, improve, influence and expand relationships with providers, community-based organizations/partners, and other stakeholders within the market and foster collaborative partnerships

You'll be rewarded and recognized for your performance in an environment that will challenge you and give you clear direction on what it takes to succeed in your role as well as provide development for other roles you may be interested in.

Required Qualifications

5+ years of experience working with FQHCs, Community Based Organizations, and State regulatory agencies
5+ years of experience working in Managed Medicaid
5+ years of experience leading operations, strategic planning, and development
Ability to handle ambiguity
Ability to travel up to 50% throughout the State of VA
Driver's License and access to reliable transportation

Preferred Qualifications

Executive level experience in dealing with multiple State Agencies
Reside in Virginia
All employees working remotely will be required to adhere to UnitedHealth Group's Telecommuter Policy

Pay is based on several factors including but not limited to local labor markets, education, work experience, certifications, etc. In addition to your salary, we offer benefits such as, a comprehensive benefits package, incentive and recognition programs, equity stock purchase and 401k contribution (all benefits are subject to eligibility requirements). No matter where or when you begin a career with us, you'll find a far-reaching choice of benefits and incentives. The salary for this role will range from $159,300 to $273,200 annually based on full-time employment. We comply with all minimum wage laws as applicable.

Application Deadline: This will be posted for a minimum of 2 business days or until a sufficient candidate pool has been collected. Job posting may come down early due to volume of applicants.

At UnitedHealth Group, our mission is to help people live healthier lives and make the health system work better for everyone. We believe everyone-of every race, gender, sexuality, age, location and income-deserves the opportunity to live their healthiest life. Today, however, there are still far too many barriers to good health which are disproportionately experienced by people of color, historically marginalized groups and those with lower incomes. We are committed to mitigating our impact on the environment and enabling and delivering equitable care that addresses health disparities and improves health outcomes - an enterprise priority reflected in our mission.

jobs.description:
Tribe 12

Administration

Full-time

Philadelphia, PA, USA

USD 110k-130k / year

Posted on Jul 20, 2026

Executive Director

Philadelphia, PA

Full-Time | Reports to the Board Chair and Executive Committee

Salary: $110,000-$130,000

Ideal Start Date: December 1, 2026

About Tribe 12

Tribe 12 connects people in their 20s and 30s to Jewish life and community in Philadelphia today so they will choose to stay connected tomorrow.

For more than fifteen years, Tribe 12 has redefined what Jewish engagement can look like by thinking expansively about how to craft community and inspire connection. Through social events, affinity groups, mentorship, learning cohorts, leadership opportunities, matchmaking, and more, we help young Jewish adults build authentic relationships, meaningful community, and lasting connections to Jewish life.

Over the past six years, Tribe 12 has nearly doubled its budget and expanded its impact, becoming one of Philadelphia's leading Jewish engagement organizations.

We believe great organizations are built by great teams. Our next Executive Director will join a dedicated and mission-driven staff, an engaged Board, and a community excited about the future.

The Opportunity

This is an opportunity to lead an innovative and dynamic organization into its next chapter.

Working closely with the Board and staff, the Executive Director will provide strategic leadership while strengthening the organization's culture, operations, partnerships, and long-term sustainability. The next Executive Director will help Tribe 12 continue asking important questions about where it can have the greatest impact and how it can best serve Jewish adults in their 20s and 30s.

We're looking for a leader who can balance vision with execution, someone who enjoys building healthy teams, developing people, creating organizational clarity, and earning the trust of staff, board members, funders, and community partners.

This role is well-suited for individuals with executive director experience and a desire to have an impact in Philadelphia’s Jewish community or candidates ready to step into their first executive director position after serving in senior leadership in a nonprofit or mission-driven organization.

What You'll Do

The Executive Director provides overall leadership for Tribe 12 by:

Leading implementation of the organization's strategic plan.
Supervising and developing a team of eight full-time and part-time employees, including three direct reports.
Building a strong organizational culture rooted in trust, accountability, collaboration, and continuous learning.
Partnering closely with the Board of Directors to strengthen governance and advance strategic priorities.
Overseeing organizational finances, budgeting, and long-term sustainability in partnership with the Treasurer and Finance Committee.
Serving as a public ambassador for Tribe 12 throughout Greater Philadelphia and beyond.
Partnering with the Senior Director for Strategy and Development to cultivate major donors and advance fundraising efforts.
Ensuring the success of fundraising events including the annual Hanukkah Fundraiser and the Jewish Philly Under 40 Awards.
Building strong relationships with funders, community organizations, volunteers, and stakeholders.
Assessing programs, operations, and organizational systems to ensure that they remain aligned with Tribe 12's mission and values.

Who You Are

Successful candidates will bring many of the following:

Significant leadership experience in a nonprofit or mission-driven organization.
Outstanding supervision, coaching, and team development skills.
Strong strategic thinking and organizational management abilities.
Experience managing budgets, projects, and organizational operations.
Comfort building relationships with boards, volunteers, donors, funders, and community partners.
Excellent communication skills, emotional intelligence, and sound judgment.
Knowledge of contemporary Jewish institutional and communal life.
Enthusiasm for helping young adults build meaningful Jewish community.
A bachelor's degree.

Fundraising experience is highly desirable. We recognize there is no single path to becoming an exceptional Executive Director and encourage candidates from a wide range of backgrounds to apply.

Work Environment

Tribe 12 offers a hybrid work environment, with staff working from our Old City Philadelphia office on Tuesdays and Thursdays and flexibility to work remotely on other weekdays.

Some evening and occasional weekend hours are required to support programs and community events.

Hiring Process

The Anticipated Search Process Includes

Initial phone interview
Two rounds of interviews with the search committee
Reference checks
Final in-person interview in Philadelphia

Reasonable travel expenses for finalists will be reimbursed.

To Apply

Please submit your resume and cover letter to hiring@tribe12.org
with the subject line: Executive Director Application

Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis and the listing will stay active until the position is filled.

Tribe 12 is an equal opportunity employer.
At Tribe 12, we value diversity and believe our organization is strongest when it reflects the richness of the communities we serve. We encourage candidates of all backgrounds and lived experiences to apply, especially those whose identities have historically been underrepresented in Jewish nonprofit leadership.

Tribe 12 does not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, citizenship, age, disability, veteran status, marital status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Tribe 12 is an equal opportunity employer.

Portland State University is Oregon’s most innovative, diverse, and affordable public research university, located in one of the nation’s most desirable places to live, and the Maseeh College of Engineering and Computer Science is your testing ground for putting new ideas into action. General Summary:
The Development Director, reporting to the Principal, serves as a key leadership team member, charged with advancing the school’s mission by securing philanthropic support. This role requires a mission-driven professional who cultivates a culture of philanthropy, champions inclusive Catholic education, and connects the unique needs of special education students with generous benefactors and community partners.

Essential Duties And Responsibilities

Fundraising & Donor Development
Design and implement a comprehensive annual development plan focusing on annual funds, major gifts, and planned giving.
Cultivate, solicit, and steward a portfolio of major donors, including parents, alumni, foundations, and local organizations.
Partner with the Principal and Board of Directors to identify and solicit leadership gifts for capital improvements and endowment funds.
Special Education Advocacy & Grant Writing
Prepare compelling, mission-focused case statements that clearly articulate the importance of Catholic special education and inclusive learning environments.
Identify, write, and manage grant proposals targeted at philanthropic foundations, corporate sponsors, and government programs that support special needs education.
Events & Community Engagement
Plan and execute signature fundraising events, such as an annual gala, golf tournament, or giving drives.
Build strategic partnerships with local businesses, community leaders, and Catholic organizations to expand the school’s visibility and resource network.
Communications & Marketing
Lead marketing and social media campaigns to highlight student achievements, school programs, and community impact.
Oversee the creation of development collateral, including newsletters, annual reports, and donor appeals.
Database & Operations
Maintain accurate records of all donor interactions and funds using constituent management software.
Ensure timely and meaningful donor acknowledgments, reporting, and tax compliance.

Knowledge, Skills, And Abilities Required

Bachelor’s Degree in Marketing, Communications, Public Relations, Business Administration or Non- Profit Administration or related field. Certified Fund-Raising Executive (CRFE) credential or equivalent preferred.
Minimum of three (3) years of development experience with verifiable successful fundraising programs and personal track record of integrity in fundraising.
Demonstrated ability to identify, cultivate, solicit, and steward individual donors, businesses, and organizations.
Familiarity and fluency with the use of technology and the ability to know how to team with others to creatively use technology in ease-of-donation/giving, development and stewardship, marketing, and media relations.
Candidate must be a practicing Roman Catholic possessing the ability to respect, promote, accommodate, and not conflict with the mission, moral and social teachings, doctrines, and laws of the Roman Catholic faith and must be a Catholic in good standing with the Church.
Must have a collaborative style; proven team builder with effective employee-relations skills. Proven ability to lead and motivate teams through recognition, participation, and delegation. A leadership style that is approachable, engaging, as well as results and action-oriented
Pursues this ministry with energy, drive, and a need to produce results, especially in the face of resistance or setbacks. Must be flexible, self-confident, assertive, and persuasive.
Required to have excellent presentation, verbal and written communication skills, skilled at public speaking. Ability to produce concise reports and recommendations, make presentations to varied groups, and articulate and inspire commitment to a shared vision.
Must be a goal oriented and self-motivated individual who can demonstrate accountability, initiative, creativity and focus in a rapidly changing and intellectually stimulating environment. Must be able to organize and prioritize work, be proactive, resolve complex problems, follow through, and simultaneously manage multiple priorities.
Ability to respect and value the cultural richness that makes up the Church and society and strives to celebrate commonalities and differences among various groups.
Exercises considerable independence and judgment with a high level of confidentiality.
Must successfully pass the required criminal background check prior to employment and maintain this clearance throughout employment.
Proficient with information technology including Microsoft Office Suite, social media usage, web site contributor level, database maintenance.

Language Skills

Ability to read, analyze, and interpret general business periodicals, professional journals, technical procedures, or governmental regulations. Ability to write reports, business correspondence, and procedure manuals. Ability to effectively present information and respond to questions from groups of managers, clients, vendors, employees, and the public.

Mathematical Skills

Ability to add, subtract, multiply, and divide in all units of measure, using whole numbers, common fractions, and decimals. Ability to compute rate, ratio, and percent and to draw and interpret bar graphs.

REASONING ABILITY

Ability to define problems, collect data, establish facts, and draw valid conclusions. Ability to interpret an extensive variety of technical instructions in mathematical or diagram form and deal with several abstract and concrete variables.

About Am Kolel

Am Kolel is an independent Jewish community dedicated to Jewish renewal by addressing spiritual needs in a welcoming, inclusive manner and responding to issues facing society with a progressive Jewish presence. Am Kolel means "an inclusive people." We celebrate the spiritual warmth of Judaism in a form that is joyful and personal. We reflect the ideals and principles of the Jewish Renewal Movement.

We are devoted to the revitalization of Judaism and the Jewish community. We offer opportunities for individuals to rediscover and discover a Judaism that is spiritually and socially meaningful; a Judaism that speaks to the heart and to the issues we face in our daily lives; a Judaism that is compassionate towards others and towards this earth.

We are a community in transition and seeking a visionary leader with experienced leadership for the years ahead. This is an opportunity to build something meaningful with a committed community.

Position Summary

The Executive Director serves as the operational and visionary leader of Am Kolel. This person reports directly to the Am Kolel Board of Directors and is responsible for all aspects of organizational management, member engagement, fundraising and community representation. This is a unique opportunity for a leader with a growth mindset. If you can identify funding opportunities, expand programming, and grow membership, you'll have the resources and freedom to build something significant.

Key Responsibilities

Leadership & Strategy

Provides strategic direction and serves as external voice for Am Kolel in collaboration with the board
Builds and maintains relationships with members, donors, volunteers, and community partners
Identifies and pursues growth opportunities (new programs, expanded reach, community partnerships)

Fundraising & Development

Develops and implements comprehensive fundraising strategy cultivating donor relationships, planning and executing giving campaigns and pursuing grant opportunities

Operations & Administration

Oversees organizational calendar, scheduling, Zoom logistics, CRM/database, and communications screening (handled by admin assistant)
Oversees the writing and distribution of weekly newsletters and special communications, and oversees website content and social media presence

Programming & Member Engagement

Organizes and consults with committees of members to develop engaging programs and build community through programming, outreach, and member engagement aligned with Am Kolel's mission

Financial Management

Oversees organizational budget and financial planning with an entrepreneurial eye toward sustainability and growth
Works with bookkeeper, treasurer, and finance committee on financial reporting
Reports monthly to the board on financial status, operational metrics, and fundraising progress

Required Qualifications & Skills

3+ years of nonprofit or community organization management experience
Strong understanding of Jewish life, practice, and community (formal Jewish training strongly preferred)
Proven ability to build community and execute engaging programming
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Proficiency with technology: email, Google Workspace, Zoom, CRM software, basic website management
Comfort with financial oversight and budget management
Ability to honor and build on the founder's vision while establishing independent leadership
Commitment to Jewish Renewal values and progressive Judaism

Added Advantages (Preferred)

Rabbinic or cantorial training/ordination
Ability to lead services, teach Jewish content, or provide spiritual direction
Musical ability (instrument, singing, music direction, or composition)

Reporting & Authority

Reports to: Am Kolel Board of Directors

Compensation & Benefits

Salary: $80,000 - $100,000/year (Commensurate with experience. Candidates with rabbinic/cantorial ordination or equivalent teaching credentials will be considered on a separate, higher compensation scale).

This is a full-time role.

Health insurance contribution
Paid time off: 3 weeks vacation + 5 personal days + major Jewish holidays
Professional development fund ($1000/year)
Flexible hybrid working arrangement; must be local to the DMV area and able to travel for in-person programming and events

The AI Access Initiative (2AI) operates within the artificial intelligence sector, focusing on leveraging AI innovations to address poverty in low- and middle-income countries. Ready to make a difference? Join us to start Caring. Connecting. Growing together

The Chief Operating Officer (COO) serves as a key member of the executive leadership team and is responsible for the day to day operational performance of the health plan, ensuring consistent execution of the Cardinal Care Managed Care Contract with the Virginia Department of Medical Assistance Services (DMAS). This role provides enterprise wide leadership across operations, clinical delivery support, network operations, compliance execution, and operational readiness-translating strategy into disciplined, reliable operations that deliver high quality, compliant, and member centered outcomes across the Commonwealth of Virginia.

The COO partners closely with the CEO, Chief Medical Officer, Chief Financial Officer, Chief Compliance Officer, and functional leaders to ensure operational excellence, audit readiness, and solid performance against contract, quality, financial, and stakeholder expectations under Cardinal Care.

You'll enjoy the flexibility to work remotely* from anywhere within the U.S., preferably in VA, as you will be required to travel 50% within the VA Market as you take on some tough challenges.

Primary Responsibilities

Operational Leadership & Execution

Leads relationship management on all Operations related areas for the Cardinal Care program with the DMAS and all other State government agencies who have regulatory oversight of elements of the Cardinal Care/DSNP program to influence program design, develop support for program efforts, and secure approvals
Lead and oversee core health plan operations (provider & member related, network, IT, claims, etc.) ensuring effective implementation of all Cardinal Care contract requirements across covered populations, regions, and services
Translate enterprise strategy and DMAS commitments into clear operational priorities, workflows, performance metrics, and accountability structures
Ensure operational consistency and scalability across regions while supporting local engagement and region specific execution aligned with DMAS expectations

Regulatory Compliance & Audit Readiness

Maintain operational readiness for DMAS, CMS, EQRO, operational, and financial audits, ensuring timely, accurate, and coordinated responses
Partner with Compliance and Legal to embed contract compliant processes, internal controls, and monitoring into daily operations
Support ongoing review and implementation of contract amendments and policy updates in coordination with DMAS

Clinical & Care Delivery Operations (Operational Oversight)

Provide operational oversight and support for care management, care coordination, behavioral health integration, LTSS operations, and population specific programs, ensuring alignment with Cardinal Care delivery models
Collaborate with the CMO and clinical leaders to ensure operational infrastructure supports quality improvement, clinical outcomes, and member experience goals

Network, Member & Provider Operations

Oversee operational functions supporting provider network performance, member services, grievances and appeals, enrollment support, and service delivery performance
Ensure operations are structured to support provider engagement, timely issue resolution, and service accessibility across all regions of the Commonwealth

Performance Management & Continuous Improvement

Establish and monitor operational KPIs, dashboards, and management routines tied to quality, compliance, financial stewardship, and stakeholder satisfaction
Lead continuous improvement initiatives to enhance efficiency, reduce operational risk, and improve service reliability while maintaining compliance

People, Culture & Cross Functional Leadership

Lead, mentor, and develop operational leaders, fostering a culture of accountability, collaboration, and operational excellence
Promote solid cross functional integration between operations, clinical, compliance, finance, and shared services

External & Stakeholder Engagement

Serve as a senior operational liaison to DMAS and other state partners as appropriate, supporting clear communication, issue resolution, and trusted relationships in partnership with the CEO and executive team
Build, develop, improve, influence and expand relationships with providers, community-based organizations/partners, and other stakeholders within the market and foster collaborative partnerships

Executive Director

Philadelphia, PA

Salary: $110,000-$130,000

Ideal Start Date: December 1, 2026

About Tribe 12

Tribe 12 connects people in their 20s and 30s to Jewish life and community in Philadelphia today so they will choose to stay connected tomorrow.

For more than fifteen years, Tribe 12 has redefined what Jewish engagement can look like by thinking expansively about how to craft community and inspire connection. Through social events, affinity groups, mentorship, learning cohorts, leadership opportunities, matchmaking, and more, we help young Jewish adults build authentic relationships, meaningful community, and lasting connections to Jewish life.

Over the past six years, Tribe 12 has nearly doubled its budget and expanded its impact, becoming one of Philadelphia's leading Jewish engagement organizations.

We believe great organizations are built by great teams. Our next Executive Director will join a dedicated and mission-driven staff, an engaged Board, and a community excited about the future.

The Opportunity

This is an opportunity to lead an innovative and dynamic organization into its next chapter.

Working closely with the Board and staff, the Executive Director will provide strategic leadership while strengthening the organization's culture, operations, partnerships, and long-term sustainability. The next Executive Director will help Tribe 12 continue asking important questions about where it can have the greatest impact and how it can best serve Jewish adults in their 20s and 30s.

We're looking for a leader who can balance vision with execution, someone who enjoys building healthy teams, developing people, creating organizational clarity, and earning the trust of staff, board members, funders, and community partners.

This role is well-suited for individuals with executive director experience and a desire to have an impact in Philadelphia’s Jewish community or candidates ready to step into their first executive director position after serving in senior leadership in a nonprofit or mission-driven organization.

What You'll Do

The Executive Director provides overall leadership for Tribe 12 by:

Leading implementation of the organization's strategic plan.
Supervising and developing a team of eight full-time and part-time employees, including three direct reports.
Building a strong organizational culture rooted in trust, accountability, collaboration, and continuous learning.
Partnering closely with the Board of Directors to strengthen governance and advance strategic priorities.
Overseeing organizational finances, budgeting, and long-term sustainability in partnership with the Treasurer and Finance Committee.
Serving as a public ambassador for Tribe 12 throughout Greater Philadelphia and beyond.
Partnering with the Senior Director for Strategy and Development to cultivate major donors and advance fundraising efforts.
Ensuring the success of fundraising events including the annual Hanukkah Fundraiser and the Jewish Philly Under 40 Awards.
Building strong relationships with funders, community organizations, volunteers, and stakeholders.
Assessing programs, operations, and organizational systems to ensure that they remain aligned with Tribe 12's mission and values.

Who You Are

Successful candidates will bring many of the following:

Significant leadership experience in a nonprofit or mission-driven organization.
Outstanding supervision, coaching, and team development skills.
Strong strategic thinking and organizational management abilities.
Experience managing budgets, projects, and organizational operations.
Comfort building relationships with boards, volunteers, donors, funders, and community partners.
Excellent communication skills, emotional intelligence, and sound judgment.
Knowledge of contemporary Jewish institutional and communal life.
Enthusiasm for helping young adults build meaningful Jewish community.
A bachelor's degree.

Fundraising experience is highly desirable. We recognize there is no single path to becoming an exceptional Executive Director and encourage candidates from a wide range of backgrounds to apply.

Work Environment

Tribe 12 offers a hybrid work environment, with staff working from our Old City Philadelphia office on Tuesdays and Thursdays and flexibility to work remotely on other weekdays.

Some evening and occasional weekend hours are required to support programs and community events.

Hiring Process

The Anticipated Search Process Includes

Initial phone interview
Two rounds of interviews with the search committee
Reference checks
Final in-person interview in Philadelphia

Reasonable travel expenses for finalists will be reimbursed.

To Apply

Please submit your resume and cover letter to hiring@tribe12.org
with the subject line: Executive Director Application

Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis and the listing will stay active until the position is filled.

Position Summary

Brief Description of PSU/School/Dept

Portland State University is Oregon’s most innovative, diverse, and affordable public research university, located in one of the nation’s most desirable places to live, and the Maseeh College of Engineering and Computer Science is your testing ground for putting new ideas into action. We believe in involving everyone—from freshmen to faculty to community—in solving the problems facing our region and our world.

Maseeh College includes five departments, 132 faculty members, and more than 2,700 students. We offer academic programs that provide transformative opportunities within the classroom and beyond. Our faculty includes six National Science Foundation (NSF) CAREER Award winners and two members of the National Academy of Engineering. When you join our college, you’ll be at the heart of an intellectually-challenging, personally rewarding, and purposefully inclusive environment, connected to Portland’s “silicon forest,” a nexus of growing, innovative industries and agencies.

Position Summary

Maseeh College of Engineering and Computer Science
seeks a collaborative, entrepreneurial, and relationship-driven leader to advance the College’s strategic partnerships and external engagement efforts. The ideal candidate is a skilled convener who builds trust across diverse constituencies, identifies opportunities for mutually beneficial collaboration, and translates strategic vision into measurable outcomes that support the College’s mission and long-term growth.

The Executive Director of Strategic Partnerships leads and advances initiatives that strengthen the College’s connections with industry, government, educational institutions, alumni, and community stakeholders. Working in close partnership with the Dean’s office leadership team, the Executive Director identifies and develops opportunities that support enrollment, workforce development, research collaborations, innovation, and community engagement. As a central coordinator for strategic initiatives, the Executive Director aligns internal priorities with external opportunities to expand the College’s impact and advance institutional goals.

This is a full-time (1.0 FTE), 12-month unclassified position reporting to the Interim Dean of Maseeh College of Engineering and Computer Science. The incumbent serves as a strategic ambassador for the College, fostering meaningful collaborations and advancing initiatives that enhance institutional visibility, strengthen external partnerships, and support the College’s long-term success.
